subject: GRB 210415A: Fermi GBM Final Real-time Localization
body:
The Fermi GBM team reports the detection of a likely LONG GRB

At 06:48:20 UT on 15 Apr 2021, the Fermi Gamma-ray Burst Monitor (GBM) triggered and located GRB 210415A (trigger 640162105.095184 / 210415284).

The on-ground calculated location, using the Fermi GBM trigger data, is RA = 130.3, Dec = -37.9 (J2000 degrees, equivalent to J2000 08h 41m, -37d 53'), with a statistical uncertainty of 11.5 degrees.

The angle from the Fermi LAT boresight is 37.0 degrees.

The skymap can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2021/bn210415284/quicklook/glg_skymap_all_bn210415284.png

The HEALPix FITS file, including the estimated localization systematic, can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2021/bn210415284/quicklook/glg_healpix_all_bn210415284.fit

The GBM light curve can be found here:
https://heasarc.gsfc.nasa.gov/FTP/fermi/data/gbm/triggers/2021/bn210415284/quicklook/glg_lc_medres34_bn210415284.gif
